title: Delete four unimported components and dead prop chain (~500 lines)
description: Grep across frontend/src finds zero imports of RagTab (123 lines), HandoffTab (124), ExecutionLogEntry (202), and AgentTabs (29) — only their own definitions match. RagTab and HandoffTab are stale older copies of the RAG section (ContextTab.tsx:266-424) and Handoff section (ContextTab.tsx:426-509); HandoffTab.tsx:23-30 even contains the OLD leak-prone socket.once handoff logic that ContextTab deliberately fixed (ContextTab.tsx:160-191 with cleanup ref + timeout), so keeping it actively misleads readers about how handoff works. ExecutionLogEntry duplicates HistoryDetailModal's MODE_LABELS/MODE_COLORS and duration formatting. Additionally: the `agents` prop is threaded TasksBoard.tsx:922 -> KanbanColumn.tsx:6/245 -> TaskCard.tsx:8 but TaskCard never reads it (it uses task.assigneeName/assigneeIcon); and TasksBoard.tsx:115 has the no-op ternary `activeBoard?.share_permission || (activeBoard ? 'admin' : 'admin')` which always yields 'admin'.
sketch: 1) `git rm` the four files. 2) Remove `agents` from TaskCard and KanbanColumn props and from the JSX at TasksBoard.tsx:922 and KanbanColumn.tsx:245. 3) Replace TasksBoard.tsx:115 with `const boardPermission = activeBoard?.share_permission || 'admin';`. Verify with `tsc` + vite build (no imports exist, so both pass).
impact: ~480 lines deleted outright, plus 3 prop-threading sites; removes a stale contradictory implementation of handoff
guidance: Sketch is accurate as written; two precision notes. (1) Keep the `agents` prop on TasksBoard itself — it is genuinely used at TasksBoard.tsx:213, 355, 806-807, 971, 1001, 1018, 1034; remove only the pass-through at line 922 plus the `agents` entries in KanbanColumn.tsx:6/245 and TaskCard.tsx:8. (2) The components are plain (not React.memo), so dropping the prop cannot alter re-render behavior — no trap there. Minor citation correction: ContextTab's fixed handoff handler spans roughly lines 152-201 (the proposal cites 160-191), with cleanup ref at 159-168 and the timeout at 185-189; substance of the claim is correct.

title: Extract shared plugin-UI module for PluginsTab and BoardPluginsTab
description: The two plugin screens are verbatim copies of each other in four places: MCP_CONNECTOR_MAP (PluginsTab.tsx:18-28 vs BoardPluginsTab.tsx:14-24, 9 connector imports duplicated in both files), getPluginMcpIds (PluginsTab.tsx:30-39 vs BoardPluginsTab.tsx:26-35), categoryColors/getCategoryClass (PluginsTab.tsx:142-151 vs BoardPluginsTab.tsx:77-85), and the assigned-plugin card with embedded connector widgets + category filter pills + available-plugin row (PluginsTab.tsx:165-307 vs BoardPluginsTab.tsx:127-238). Today adding a new MCP connector (e.g. a Notion plugin) requires editing two files identically; missing one silently breaks one of the surfaces.
sketch:
New `frontend/src/components/plugins/pluginShared.tsx` exporting:
- `MCP_CONNECTOR_MAP: Record<string, ComponentType<{agentId?; boardId?; onStatusChange}>>`
- `getPluginMcpIds(plugin): string[]`
- `getCategoryClass(cat: string): string`
- `<CategoryFilterPills categories value onChange />`
- `<AssignedPluginCard plugin connectorProps onRemove extraActions?>{badges}</AssignedPluginCard>` (renders icon/name/category/MCP badges + connector widgets keyed off connectorProps `{agentId}` or `{boardId}`)
- `<AvailablePluginRow plugin onAdd addLabel badges? />`
PluginsTab keeps only its activation-modal flow and the partagé/privé/auth-requise badges (passed as children); BoardPluginsTab keeps only its load/assign/remove state.
impact: ~150 duplicated lines removed; new connectors registered once; both files drop under 220 lines
guidance: Corrections and traps: (1) The description's "verbatim copies in four places" overstates the fourth — the assigned-card/available-row JSX shares identical wrappers but diverges in badges (partagé/privé, auth requise), the KeyRound "Modifier mes accès" button (PluginsTab.tsx:193-199), needsCreds button label, handleAssign(plugin) vs handleAssign(plugin.id), connector props, and empty-state text; the sketch's props/children design handles this, but implement it as parameterization, not unification. (2) Badge DOM-order trap in AvailablePluginRow: in PluginsTab the partagé/privé badge renders BEFORE the MCP-count badge (267-278) and "auth requise" AFTER it (284-288) — a single badges slot cannot preserve order; use two slots (beforeMcp/afterMcp) or let the call site render the MCP badge. Same trap in AssignedPluginCard: the "partagé" badge sits between category and MCP badges (180-189). (3) Keep connectors keyed by mcpId in both surfaces to avoid remounting OAuth widgets with in-flight state. (4) Do not retype MCP_CONNECTOR_MAP as ComponentType<{agentId?; boardId?; onStatusChange}> unless all 9 connectors' prop types accept optional ids — keep the existing Record<string, any> for this pass; TS strictness is out of scope. (5) Preserve the differing error handling: PluginsTab's handleAssign/handleRemove set actionError while BoardPluginsTab's have no try/catch — leave both as-is in call sites. (6) Bonus: also point BroadcastPanel.tsx:9-17 (third verbatim categoryColors/getCategoryClass copy) at the new shared module; moving it from component-body scope to module scope is behavior-identical since it is a pure object. (7) pluginNeedsCredentials and the activation-modal flow must stay PluginsTab-only as sketched. (8) Impact claim "both files under 220 lines" is optimistic for PluginsTab (347 lines, modal stays; ~250-270 realistic) — immaterial to the decision.

title: useBoardRepos / useBoardStorages hooks for the thrice-duplicated plugin-resource fetch
description: The pattern "if no boardId clear list; else fetch api.getBoardAvailableRepos/Storages, store list, store error message on failure" is copy-pasted: repos at TaskDetailModal.tsx:52-59, CreateTaskModal.tsx:33-39, AgentDetail.tsx:78-85; storages at TaskDetailModal.tsx:62-69 and CreateTaskModal.tsx:56-62. Each call site re-declares the same two useState pairs (availableRepos/repoLoadError etc.). Only AgentDetail has the `cancelled` guard against late responses after board switch — the other four copies silently lack it, an inconsistency a shared hook would erase.
impact: ~45 lines removed across 3 files; cancellation behavior becomes uniform; 4 useState pairs disappear from TaskDetailModal/CreateTaskModal
guidance: Corrections and traps for the implementer: (1) The AgentDetail copy differs in more than the cancelled guard — it also normalizes the response with `Array.isArray(repos) ? repos : []` and swallows errors (no error state). The hook should keep the Array.isArray normalization (strictly defensive) and always track error; AgentDetail simply ignores the returned `error`, preserving its silent-failure behavior. (2) Preserve distinct fallback messages per resource: 'Failed to load repos' vs 'Failed to load storages'. (3) In the modal copies, the falsy-boardId early return does NOT clear a previously-set error (e.g. TaskDetailModal.tsx:54 returns before line 55's setRepoLoadError(null)); a hook will naturally clear it — acceptable normalization since modal boardIds never transition set→unset mid-lifetime, but make it deliberate, not accidental. (4) Adding the cancelled guard to the four modal copies is a behavior change in the strict sense (late responses after a boardId change are now dropped instead of overwriting fresh state); it fixes a latent race and should be named as such in the commit message. (5) CreateTaskModal's dependent effects (lines 42-49, 65-72) and handleSubmit (line 103) must consume the hook's returned arrays under the same local names — `const { repos: availableRepos, error: repoLoadError } = useBoardRepos(boardId)` — so no other lines change. (6) Optional tightening: implement one generic `useBoardList(boardId, fetcher, fallbackMsg)` internally with `useBoardRepos`/`useBoardStorages` as two-line wrappers, so the hook body itself isn't duplicated. Note frontend/src/hooks/ does not exist yet; creating it for this is fine.

title: Shared RecurrenceFields component + recurrence payload/label helpers
description: Three duplications around recurrence: (1) the editor form — period select, custom-minutes input, purge-after-days input — is near-verbatim in TaskDetailModal.tsx:606-649 and CreateTaskModal.tsx:278-321 (same labels, same clamping `Math.max(0, Math.min(3650, parseInt(...)))`); (2) the payload construction `{enabled, period, intervalMinutes: custom ? n : RECURRENCE_PERIODS.find(...).minutes || 1440, historyRetentionDays: d > 0 ? d : null}` is duplicated at TaskDetailModal.tsx:174-181 and CreateTaskModal.tsx:94-101; (3) TaskDetailModal.tsx:559-565 re-implements period display labels as a 5-branch ternary chain ('hourly' ? 'Every hour' : ...) instead of looking up RECURRENCE_PERIODS, so adding a period means touching 3+ places.
impact: ~85 duplicated lines removed; period definitions live in exactly one table
guidance: Two behavior-preservation traps to respect: (1) ACCENT COLOR — the duplicated forms are NOT pixel-identical: TaskDetailModal uses focus:border-teal-500 (lines 614/629/645), CreateTaskModal uses focus:border-indigo-500 (lines 285/300/318). RecurrenceFields must take an accent prop (e.g. focusClass or accent='teal'|'indigo') or the visual identity changes in one modal. (2) DISABLED-BRANCH WIRE FORMAT — the else branches differ deliberately: TaskDetailModal.tsx:181 sends `{ enabled: false }` (explicit disable on updateTask PATCH) while CreateTaskModal.tsx:101 sends `undefined` (field omitted from the positional addTask call at line 105). buildRecurrence must build ONLY the enabled-true payload; drop the `enabled` param from the sketched signature and keep each call site's own ternary (`recEnabled ? buildRecurrence(...) : { enabled: false }` / `recurring ? buildRecurrence(...) : undefined`). Unifying the falsy branch would change the addTask request body. Minor: copy the intervalMinutes expression verbatim (`custom ? n : RECURRENCE_PERIODS.find(...)?.minutes || 1440`) to preserve the `|| 1440` falsy fallback ordering, and keep recurrenceLabel's custom-check before the table lookup so 'Custom interval' (the table label for custom) is never displayed. taskConstants.ts is loosely typed JS-in-TS; matching that style avoids tsc friction.

title: TaskDetailModal: collapse five copy-pasted inline-edit metadata rows into an EditableSelectRow
description: TaskDetailModal (1253 lines, ~30 useState hooks) renders five metadata rows that are the same widget copy-pasted: Repo (697-748), Storage (750-801), Type (803-869), Assignee (871-940), Board (942-992). Each repeats: an editingX boolean, a savingX boolean, a label row with lucide icon, a view mode (colored badge or italic 'None' + Edit3 pencil), and an edit mode (autoFocus select with defaultValue + onChange-saves + X cancel button). The save handlers handleRepoChange (134-150) and handleStorageChange (152-168) are also structurally identical, and Type/Assignee inline their save logic inside JSX onChange (814-828, 882-896), which is why the file reads as a wall. 10 of the 30 state hooks exist only to serve these rows.
impact: ~230 lines removed from TaskDetailModal (1253 -> ~1000), 10 state hooks eliminated, all five rows guaranteed to behave identically
guidance: 1) Exclude the Board row (942-992) from EditableSelectRow, or support its confirm-flow explicitly: its select onChange does NOT save (sets boardMoveTarget → confirmation modal 1194-1235; actual save in handleBoardMoveConfirm 205-221 which also calls onClose), the select stays open behind the modal, disabled={task.actionRunning} not a saving flag, no empty option, defaultValue falls back to activeBoardId, cancel resets editingBoard AND boardMoveTarget. Extract only Repo/Storage/Type/Assignee. 2) Let each row's onSave wrapper own the no-op guard and refresh, because they differ: repo/storage `await onRefresh()` before closing edit; type/assignee fire `onRefresh?.()` un-awaited. And the guards are not equivalent: repo/storage compare `(v || null) === (task.x || null)` (134-135, 152-153) while type/assignee compare `null === (task.x || '')` (816, 884), meaning selecting None/Unassigned when already empty currently DOES hit the API (updateTask {taskType:''} / setTaskAssignee(null)). Do not normalize the guard inside the shared component — replicate per-row, or change it only as a deliberate, called-out fix. 3) The pencil-replacement error states differ and must be a prop: repo shows amber 'github plugin off' with title={repoLoadError} (735-736); storage shows dark 'No drive connected' without title (788-789); type/assignee always show the pencil. 4) Select disabled conditions differ: repo/storage disable when saving OR options list empty (709, 762); type/assignee only while saving (829, 897). 5) Preserve cosmetics per-row via props: storage uses amber border/hover accent vs indigo elsewhere; widths none vs w-36; option key shapes (storage key `${s.provider}:${s.path}`); assignee badge is clickable (onNavigateToAgent + onClose, 920-926) and its empty label is 'Unassigned' not 'None'. 6) Keep the uncontrolled `defaultValue` + autoFocus select semantics; do not switch to a controlled value. 7) Correct the impact claims: file is 1213 lines (not 1253); with Board excluded, savings are ~150-180 lines and 8 state hooks (not 230/10).

title: TasksBoard: extract one optimistic moveTaskToColumn used by drop, touch-drop, and batch move
description: handleDrop (496-551), handleTouchDrop (554-598) and handleBatchMove (601-620) re-implement the same fragile optimistic-update protocol: find task / guard actionRunning / inFlightTaskIds.current.add / optimistic setDbTasks status change / updateTaskById({column}) / apply server response / revert to prevStatus on error / setTimeout 2000ms in-flight release. The subtle clock-skew rationale is written once at 518-522 and then referenced by 'See handleDrop' comments at 573 and 605 — a sign the logic itself should be shared, not the comment. handleDrop and handleTouchDrop differ only in payload parsing and where the card is inserted (dropIdx vs append).
impact: ~70 lines removed; the clock-skew/in-flight invariants are encoded once instead of three commented copies; TasksBoard drops toward 1000 lines
guidance: Scope the helper to handleDrop + handleTouchDrop only. (1) Keep handleTouchDrop's same-column no-op: it must early-return (or pass insertIdx undefined) before reaching the helper's same-column branch, because it always supplies a defined insertIdx (tasksByColumn[col.id].length) and the sketched helper would otherwise turn a same-column touch drop into a reorder-to-end plus a reorderTasks API call — current behavior is a silent no-op (lines 569-570). (2) Do NOT map handleBatchMove over the per-task helper: it has no per-task actionRunning guard (helper would skip running tasks), its error path is refreshAll() not per-task status revert, Promise.all over raw updateTaskById is fail-fast while the error-swallowing helper never rejects, and the helper's success-path refreshAll() would fire once per task instead of once total. The shared optimistic/in-flight bracket there is ~6 lines; leave handleBatchMove untouched. (3) Keep an outer try/catch in the callers (or helper) — current code at 502-550/556-597 swallows synchronous throws from lookup/reorderColumnTasks; the sketch's helper has no outer catch, so a throw in the same-column reorder branch would become an unhandled rejection. (4) Console labels differ ('Drop API failed' vs 'Touch drop API failed'); pass a label param if log greppability matters. (5) isReadOnly guard stays in callers (helper signature lacks it). Impact restated: ~40-45 lines, not ~70, since batch move stays.

title: TaskDetailModal: extract TaskTimeline and unify history-entry labels with HistoryDetailModal
description: TaskDetailModal.tsx:995-1153 is a 158-line IIFE inside JSX that builds the merged history+commit timeline and renders each entry via a six-branch nested ternary chain (1079-1141: execution/edit/reassign/error/stopped/status-change, each picking its own icon + color + label). HistoryDetailModal.tsx:56-82 (renderEntryIcon/renderEntryTitle) plus its MODE_LABELS/MODE_COLORS tables (11-12) encode the same entry-type -> icon/label/color mapping a second time, and TaskDetailModal line 1083 re-implements MODE_LABELS as an inline ternary (`item.h.mode === 'refine' ? 'Refine' : item.h.mode === 'decide' ? 'Decide' : ...`). Two parallel renderings of the same domain concept guarantee drift (e.g. the 'restored' type exists in HistoryDetailModal:62 but not in the TaskDetailModal timeline).
sketch:
1) Move MODE_LABELS/MODE_COLORS into taskConstants.ts and add `HISTORY_ENTRY_META: Record<type, {Icon, color, label(entry)}>` covering execution/edit/reassign/error/stopped/restored/status.
2) New `tasks/TaskTimeline.tsx` with props `{history, commits, onOpenCommit, onRemoveCommit, onOpenEntry, createdAt}` containing the merge-and-sort (current lines 996-1006) and per-entry row rendering as a lookup over HISTORY_ENTRY_META instead of the ternary chain.
3) HistoryDetailModal's renderEntryIcon/renderEntryTitle become thin wrappers over HISTORY_ENTRY_META.
impact: TaskDetailModal loses ~160 lines; one source of truth for entry icons/labels/mode names; new history types automatically consistent in both views
guidance: Narrow the scope. KEEP: (a) extract TaskTimeline.tsx from TaskDetailModal.tsx:995-1153 with props {history, commits, createdAt, onOpenEntry, onOpenCommit (accepting hash|null to cover both the per-commit click at 1040 and the 'View all diffs' button at 1026), onRemoveCommit (wrapping the async api.removeTaskCommit -> onRefresh at 1052-1055)}; (b) move MODE_LABELS into taskConstants.ts and replace the inline ternary at TaskDetailModal:1083 with MODE_LABELS[item.h.mode || 'execute'] || 'Execution' (exactly equivalent). DROP: the shared HISTORY_ENTRY_META consumed by both views. Reasons it breaks behavior preservation: (1) execution label color — timeline is success ? text-blue-300 : text-red-300 for ALL modes (line 1082) but the modal uses MODE_COLORS[mode] (violet for refine, amber for decide, teal for title, pink for set_type; HistoryDetailModal:71); a single color field restyles one view. (2) labels and label colors diverge by design — timeline: 'edited {fields}' in text-dark-200 (1093), 'reassigned' in text-dark-200 (1101), error snippet .slice(0,80) (1117), msg counts (1086-1088), from -> status with ArrowRight (1130-1136); modal: 'Field edited' in amber-300, 'Reassigned' in indigo-300, 'Status change' (HistoryDetailModal:76-81). A single label(entry) cannot serve both; a two-variant meta table is over-abstraction. (3) adding 'restored' to the timeline is a visible UI change: restored entries currently render via the default status-change branch (showing their status field, see api/src/services/agentManager/tasks.ts:553); under the meta they'd render a teal RotateCcw 'restored' row — the proposal's 'automatically consistent in both views' impact is a behavior change, not a refactor. (4) icon table is not fully shared either: timeline default branch has no leading icon; modal default uses ArrowRight dark-400; modal has RotateCcw for restored, timeline has none. Extraction traps: preserve concat order [...historyItems, ...commitItems] before the stable sort so equal timestamps keep history-before-commit ordering; preserve React keys h-${i} and c-${c.hash || i}; keep the empty-timeline 'Created' fallback (1007-1019); keep the entries' missing-date fallback (treats falsy at as epoch 0, lines 1003-1004). Optionally TaskTimeline may internally use a lookup table instead of the ternary chain — fine, as long as it is private to the timeline and reproduces the exact per-branch markup. MODE_COLORS only has one consumer (HistoryDetailModal); moving it to taskConstants is optional and adds nothing.

title: PermissionsTab: extract buildPerms/buildHookRules and a markDirty helper
description: Two non-trivial merge expressions are duplicated verbatim: the 4-section DEFAULT_PERMISSIONS spread-merge appears at the useState initializer (184-191) and again in the agent.id effect (211-218); the BUILTIN_RULES overlay merge (`BUILTIN_RULES.map(builtin => override ? {...builtin, ...override} : {...builtin}).concat(existing.filter(...))`) appears at 200-206 and 221-227. If a fifth permission section is added, both copies must change identically or initial mount and agent-switch diverge. Also the pair `setHasChanges(true); setSaved(false);` is repeated in five handlers (237-238, 281, 286, 295, 300), and `!BUILTIN_RULES.find(b => b.id === ...)` does an O(n) scan per rule render (501).
impact: ~30 lines removed; mount and agent-switch initialization provably identical; rule-origin check becomes a Set lookup
guidance: Sketch is correct as-is. Four traps to respect: (1) keep useState initializers lazy — useState(() => buildPerms(agent)) / useState(() => buildHookRules(agent)) — eager calls would re-run the merges every render; (2) do NOT route the effect's reset (lines 228-229: setHasChanges(false); setSaved(false)) or handleSave's setSaved(true); setHasChanges(false) (248-249) through markDirty — different semantics; markDirty applies only to the five cited handlers; (3) buildPerms must retain the top-level ...agent.permissions spread before the four section merges so unknown extra permission keys survive the save round-trip via api.updateAgent; (4) keep params loosely typed ((agent), (r: any)) consistent with the file's untyped-props style so frontend tsc stays at 0 errors. Set-vs-find at line 501 is semantically equivalent (string ids, truthy objects). Also reuse BUILTIN_RULE_IDS inside buildHookRules' filter, replacing both find-based scans.

title: ChatTab: one image-ingestion helper for file picker and clipboard paste
description: The image policy (accepted mime regex `^image\/(png|jpeg|gif|webp)$`, 10MB cap, FileReader -> dataURL -> `^data:(image\/[^;]+);base64,(.+)$` extraction -> onAddImages([{mediaType, data, preview}])) is implemented twice: handleFileSelect at ChatTab.tsx:238-261 and the textarea onPaste handler inlined in JSX at ChatTab.tsx:478-499. The paste copy is buried 25 lines deep inside the textarea props, making the input JSX hard to scan, and any policy change (new format, size limit) must be applied twice.
impact: ~30 lines removed; the textarea JSX becomes readable; one place defines accepted formats/size
guidance: Two corrections to the sketch, both behavior-preservation traps: (1) preventDefault ordering — the original onPaste (line 481-483) calls e.preventDefault() when any clipboard ITEM has an image mime, BEFORE getAsFile(); the sketch defers it until after getAsFile() null-filtering, changing the edge case where getAsFile() returns null. Keep original order: filter imageItems by item.type, early-return if empty, preventDefault(), then map to files and call ingestImageFiles. (2) In the helper's onload, keep the existing `if (typeof dataUrl !== 'string') return;` guard verbatim instead of the sketch's String() coercion, and use dataUrl as preview — this matches both originals exactly and keeps TS narrowing. Also preserve: the `if (!supportsImages) return;` early return at the top of onPaste; `e.target.value = ''` reset only in handleFileSelect; and the per-file single-element onAddImages([{...}]) calls (do not batch files into one call — readers complete asynchronously and out of order). The mime re-check on file.type inside the helper for pasted files is a harmless redundancy (File.type equals the DataTransferItem.type for clipboard images). Optional bonus, not required: derive the input accept attr (line 402) from the same const list so the format policy truly lives in one place.

title: ChatMessage: extract the triplicated truncate ('Restart from here') button
description: The exact same 9-line button — `{!isLast && onTruncate && (<button onClick={() => onTruncate(index - 1)} className="absolute -right-1 top-1 opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 ..." title="Restart from here"><Scissors/></button>)}` — appears three times in the same file: in the tool-result branch (195-204), the delegation-task branch (232-241), and the default branch (312-320). The 200-char className is repeated character-for-character.
impact: ~20 lines removed; styling/behavior of the truncate affordance defined once
guidance: Sketch is correct as-is. Three constraints to respect: (1) define TruncateButton at module scope (like DelegationCallBlock), not inside ChatMessage, to avoid per-render remounts; (2) keep it rendered inside each `group relative` wrapper — group-hover visibility and absolute positioning depend on that ancestor; (3) keep the full className as one static string literal so Tailwind's content scanner still sees the classes. Optionally pass {isLast, onTruncate, index} and guard inside the component (`if (isLast || !onTruncate) return null`) instead of a `show` prop — equivalent and slightly less boilerplate at the three call sites.

title: api.addTask: replace 11 positional params with an options object
description: api.ts:254 declares `addTask(agentId, text, status, boardId, repoFullName, recurrence, taskType, isManual, repoProvider = 'github', storagePath = null, storageProvider = 'onedride')`-style with 11 positional params. The two call sites show the cost: CreateTaskModal.tsx:105 passes an unreadable run of 11 mixed values where `'github'` is hardcoded in position 9 (even though availableRepos carries a per-repo provider — TaskDetailModal.tsx:139-141 derives it properly, so the inconsistency is invisible at the call site); AllCommitsDiffModal.tsx:327 passes EIGHT consecutive `undefined`s. The wire format (POST body) is already a named object built inside addTask, so this is purely an internal signature fix.
sketch:
```ts
addTask: (agentId, text, opts: { status?; boardId?; repoFullName?; repoProvider?; recurrence?; taskType?; isManual?; storagePath?; storageProvider? } = {}) => apiFetch(..., { body: JSON.stringify({ text, ...(opts.status && {status: opts.status}), ... }) })
```
CreateTaskModal: `api.addTask(defaultAgentId, trimmed, { status, boardId, repoFullName: repoFullName || undefined, repoProvider: availableRepos.find(r => r.fullName === repoFullName)?.provider, recurrence, taskType: taskType || undefined, isManual: isManual || undefined, storagePath: storagePath || null, storageProvider })`. AllCommitsDiffModal: `api.addTask(agentId, taskText)`. (Keeping repoProvider 'github' as the default preserves behavior; deriving it is an optional one-line follow-up.)
impact: Both call sites become self-describing; eliminates the 8x-undefined call; positional-ordering bugs become impossible
guidance: 1) The sketch contradicts its own parenthetical: the proposed CreateTaskModal call passes `repoProvider: availableRepos.find(r => r.fullName === repoFullName)?.provider`, which DERIVES the provider — a behavior change to the POST body for non-github repos (current code always sends 'github'). For the behavior-preserving refactor, pass `repoProvider: 'github'` explicitly (or omit it and rely on the default); do the derivation as a separate follow-up commit if the owner wants the bug fix. 2) Keep the body construction at api.ts:258-267 verbatim; just destructure: `const { status, boardId, repoFullName, recurrence, taskType, isManual, repoProvider = 'github', storagePath = null, storageProvider = 'onedrive' } = opts;`. In particular preserve the coupling that repoProvider/storageProvider only ship when repoFullName/storagePath are truthy (`...(repoFullName && { repoFullName, repoProvider })`). 3) Delete the now-stale comment at AllCommitsDiffModal.tsx:324-326 ("api.addTask declares ... required params ... passing undefined is runtime-identical") when the call becomes `api.addTask(agentId, taskText)`. 4) Minor: the proposal typos the default as 'onedride'; actual code is 'onedrive'. 5) CreateTaskModal must keep passing `storagePath: storagePath || null` and its computed `storageProvider` (lines 102-104) unchanged.

title: TerminalTab: move Claude OAuth link reconstruction into its own module
description: TerminalTab.tsx is 735 lines mixing two unrelated concerns: the PTY/WebSocket/resize lifecycle (the actual terminal) and ~135 lines of Claude OAuth URL reconstruction (constants at 41-43, openExternalLink 95-103, getTerminalLine/firstNonWhitespaceIndex/buildClaudeOAuthLink/createClaudeOAuthLinkProvider/reconstructClaudeOAuthUrlFromBuffer at 105-175). A reader tracing a reconnect bug has to scroll past pure link-parsing code first. Notably ChatMessage.tsx:118-148 contains a second, independent implementation of the same concept (reconstructWrappedOAuthUrls, joining a wrapped claude.com/cai/oauth URL across lines) for plain text instead of the xterm buffer — colocating both in one module makes the parallel visible and keeps the CLAUDE_OAUTH_PREFIX constant single-sourced.
sketch: New `frontend/src/components/agentDetail/claudeOAuthLinks.ts` exporting `CLAUDE_OAUTH_PREFIX`, `openExternalLink(uri)`, `createClaudeOAuthLinkProvider(term)`, `reconstructClaudeOAuthUrlFromBuffer(term, uri)` (xterm flavor) and `reconstructWrappedOAuthUrlsInText(text)` (moved from ChatMessage). TerminalTab imports the three xterm helpers (used at lines 375-383); ChatMessage imports the text flavor in linkifyRawUrls (line 153). No behavior change — pure relocation.
impact: TerminalTab drops to ~590 lines focused on terminal lifecycle; the two OAuth-rewrapping implementations live side by side with one shared prefix constant; helpers become unit-testable
guidance: Behavior-preservation traps for the implementer: (1) Do NOT unify the two implementations — they differ deliberately. The xterm flavor caps continuation at CLAUDE_OAUTH_MAX_CONTINUATION_LINES=32 (TerminalTab.tsx:124-128) and gates on the https-only CLAUDE_OAUTH_PREFIX via indexOf/includes (lines 114, 156, 165, 170); the text flavor (ChatMessage.tsx:118-148) is uncapped (consumes lines until blank/EOF) and matches an `https?` regex with no prefix gate, so it rewraps http:// URLs the xterm flavor ignores. Colocate only. (2) Do not derive the `https?:\/\/claude\.com\/cai\/oauth\/authorize\?code=\S*` regexes from CLAUDE_OAUTH_PREFIX or vice versa — the constant is https-only while both regexes accept http; deriving either direction changes matching behavior. (3) Keep the `typeof text !== 'string'` guard in the moved text function (ChatMessage.tsx:119) — callers pass seg.content which may be non-string and tsconfig is non-strict, so there is no compile-time protection. (4) Move all three constants (lines 41-43) plus the private helpers getTerminalLine/firstNonWhitespaceIndex/buildClaudeOAuthLink into the new module; they can stay unexported there. (5) Minor description inaccuracy, not a blocker: CLAUDE_OAUTH_PREFIX is not currently duplicated in ChatMessage (it uses an inline regex, not the constant), so "single-sourced constant" overstates the dedup — exporting it from the new module changes nothing for ChatMessage and the import should not replace ChatMessage's regex.
